tasks.name: Make a one-time $45 donation to the program that feeds the most children per $10
tasks.description: Donate exactly $45 as a one-time gift to a child hunger program. Among the listed programs, select the one that shows the highest "children fed per $10" statistic, and complete the donation using a credit/debit card while opting to cover processing fees.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Donate" link in the main header navigation.
On the donation page, click the "One-time donation" tab.

In the list of featured programs (e.g., "School Lunches", "Emergency Food Boxes", "Maternal Nutrition"), compare the displayed "children fed per $10" numbers and click the "Give Now" button on the program with the highest value.
On the amount selection area, click into the custom amount field and type "45" as the donation amount.
Check the checkbox labeled "Cover processing fees" or similar to add fee coverage to the donation.
In the payment method section, select the "Credit/Debit Card" option.
Fill in the card payment form with sample data (e.g., name, card number, expiration date, CVV, and billing address) in all required fields.
Click the final "Complete Donation" or equivalent button to submit the one-time $45 donation to the selected program.

tasks.name: Set up a $25 monthly donation to the country with the highest listed child malnutrition rate
tasks.description: Create a recurring monthly donation of exactly $25. From the country list showing malnutrition percentages, choose the country with the highest percentage, set the charge date to the 15th of each month, and opt in for SMS updates before submitting payment details.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Donate" link in the main header navigation.
On the donation page, click the "Monthly donation" or "Give monthly" tab.
In the region or country dropdown, open the list of countries that show child malnutrition percentages and select the country with the highest percentage value displayed.
In the monthly amount field, type "25" to set a $25 recurring donation.
Open the "Charge date" or "Donation date" dropdown and select the 15th of the month as the recurring charge date.
Check the checkbox labeled "Send me SMS updates" (or similar) and enter a sample phone number such as "555-123-4567" in the phone number field.
In the payment method area, select "Credit/Debit Card" as the payment option.
Fill in all required cardholder and billing information fields with sample data (name, card number, expiration, CVV, address).
Click the "Start Monthly Donation" or equivalent button to submit the $25 monthly donation to the selected country.

tasks.name: Register for the earliest high-need Saturday volunteer event within 20 miles of ZIP 94103 next month
tasks.description: Find a weekend meal-packing volunteer event occurring on a Saturday next month within 20 miles of ZIP code 94103. Among events labeled with "High need", choose the earliest date and register one adult volunteer.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Get Involved" menu in the header and select "Volunteer" from the dropdown.
On the volunteer page, locate the location search field and type "94103" as the ZIP code.
Adjust the distance or radius filter to "20 miles" using the slider or dropdown and apply the filter.
Open the date filter and select the option for "Next month" to limit results to that time period.
In the day-of-week filter, select "Saturday" so only Saturday events are shown.
In the need level or priority filter, select the "High need" checkbox and apply the filters so only high-need events appear.
Ensure the events are sorted by date (earliest first) using the sort dropdown if available, then click on the first event card in the filtered list.
On the event detail page, click the "Register" or "Sign up" button.
In the registration form, select or enter "1" adult participant, fill in the required contact fields (e.g., name and email), and click the "Complete Registration" or equivalent button.

tasks.name: Start a $39/month sponsorship for the longest-waiting child aged 6–10 in Central America
tasks.description: Sponsor one child with a monthly commitment of $39. Filter profiles to ages 6–10 in the Central America region, sort by longest waiting time, and start a sponsorship for the first child in that sorted list.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Sponsor a Child" link in the main header navigation.
On the sponsorship search page, use the age filter controls to select an age range of 6 to 10 years and apply the filter.
Use the region or location filter to select "Central America" and apply this filter.
Open the sort dropdown and choose the option that sorts by "Longest waiting" or equivalent (e.g., "Days waiting – High to Low").
From the sorted list of child profiles, click on the first child profile card shown.
On the child's profile page, click the button labeled "Sponsor for $39/month" or similar.
On the sponsorship form, verify the frequency is set to "Monthly" if needed, then type "39" in the monthly amount field if it is not already set.
Fill in all required sponsor information fields (such as full name, email address, and mailing address).
Click the "Start Sponsorship" or equivalent button to begin the $39/month sponsorship for the selected child.

tasks.name: Register an adult and a child for the earliest Family 5K run in Chicago in June
tasks.description: Sign up two participants (one adult and one child) for a Family 5K fundraising event. Filter events to Chicago in June, choose the earliest "Family 5K" event, select the earliest start time offered, and complete registration for both participants.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Events" link in the main header navigation.
On the events page, use the event type filter to select "Run/Walk" and apply the filter.
Open the location dropdown and select "Chicago" as the city.
Use the date range selector to set the start date to June 1 and the end date to June 30 of the current year, then apply the date filter.
From the filtered list of events, locate an event whose title includes "Family 5K" and click on the earliest dated "Family 5K" event shown.
On the event detail page, open the start time dropdown and select the earliest available start time option.
Click the "Register" or "Sign Up" button for the event.
In the registration form, add one adult participant and one child participant by selecting the appropriate participant types and entering the required details (e.g., names and ages) for both.
Click the "Continue" or "Complete Registration" button to submit the registration for the two participants.

tasks.name: Create a personal 'Summer Meals 2025' fundraiser page with a $500 goal and copied share link
tasks.description: Set up a peer-to-peer fundraising page under the "Summer Meals 2025" campaign with a $500 goal, a custom title and URL, and a description of at least 40 words, then use the on-site "Copy link" feature to copy the shareable page URL.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click the "Fundraise" link in the main header navigation.
On the fundraising campaigns page, locate and click the campaign card titled "Summer Meals 2025".
On the campaign page, click the "Create Fundraising Page" or similar button.
In the goal amount field, type "500" to set a $500 fundraising goal.
In the page title field, type "Alex's Summer Meals Fundraiser" as the fundraising page title.
In the story or description text area, type at least two sentences totaling at least 40 words explaining why raising $500 for summer meals is important.
In the custom URL or page link field, type "alex-summer-meals-500" as the unique URL ending.
Click the "Create Page" or equivalent button to finalize the fundraising page.
On the confirmation or dashboard screen for your new page, click the "Copy link" button located next to the personal fundraiser URL.

tasks.name: Request a postal and email info kit for an African school meal program serving at least 50,000 children
tasks.description: Find a school meal program in Africa that serves at least 50,000 children and request an information kit to be delivered by both postal mail and email by completing the on-site request form.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Hover over or click the "Our Work" menu in the header and select "Programs" from the dropdown.
On the programs page, use the program type filter to select "School Meal Programs" and apply it.
Open the region filter dropdown and select "Africa", then apply the filter.
Use the impact or reach filter to set the minimum number of children served to "50,000" and apply this filter.
From the filtered list of programs, click on the first program card that appears.
On the program detail page, click the "Request Info Kit" or similar button.
In the info kit request form, select the delivery method option labeled "Postal mail + email" or an equivalent combined method.
Fill in all required fields such as full name, email address, and complete postal mailing address.
Click the "Submit Request" or equivalent button to send the info kit request for the selected program.

tasks.name: Send an advocacy letter on school breakfast funding to representatives for ZIP 10001 with added custom text
tasks.description: Use the site's advocacy tool to send a letter about increasing school breakfast funding to the representatives for ZIP code 10001. Choose the relevant topic, add at least 20 custom words to the first paragraph, and select both email and print-ready PDF delivery options before submitting.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Hover over or click the "Take Action" menu in the header and select "Advocate" from the dropdown.
On the advocacy page, click the tool or button labeled "Write Your Representative" or similar.
In the ZIP code field, type "10001" and click the button to find officials or load the matching representatives.
From the topic dropdown, select the option titled "Increase school breakfast funding" or the closest equivalent.
In the letter editor, click into the first paragraph of the pre-written letter and type at least 20 additional words explaining why school breakfasts are important for children.
Scroll to the delivery options section and check both the "Send email through this site" and "Generate print-ready PDF" checkboxes if available.
Fill in all required contact fields such as full name, email address, street address, city, and state.
Click the "Send Letter" or equivalent button to submit the advocacy message using the selected delivery options.
